paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Observe the month of Abib, and keep the passover unto Jehovah thy God; for in the month of Abib Jehovah thy God brought thee forth out of Egypt by night.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And thou shalt sacrifice the passover unto Jehovah thy God, of the flock and the herd, in the place which Jehovah shall choose, to cause his name to dwell there.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou shalt eat no leavened bread with it; seven days shalt thou eat unleavened bread therewith, even the bread of affliction; for thou camest forth out of the land of Egypt in haste: that thou mayest remember the day when thou camest forth out of the land of Egypt all the days of thy life.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And there shall be no leaven seen with thee in all thy borders seven days; neither shall any of the flesh, which thou sacrificest the first day at even, remain all night until the morning.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou mayest not sacrifice the passover within any of thy gates, which Jehovah thy God giveth thee;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: but at the place which Jehovah thy God shall choose, to cause his name to dwell in, there thou shalt sacrifice the passover at even, at the going down of the sun, at the season that thou camest forth out of Egypt.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And thou shalt roast and eat it in the place which Jehovah thy God shall choose: and thou shalt turn in the morning, and go unto thy tents.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Six days thou shalt eat unleavened bread; and on the seventh day shall be a solemn assembly to Jehovah thy God; thou shalt do no work [therein].

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Seven weeks shalt thou number unto thee: from the time thou beginnest to put the sickle to the standing grain shalt thou begin to number seven weeks.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And thou shalt keep the feast of weeks unto Jehovah thy God with a tribute of a freewill-offering of thy hand, which thou shalt give, according as Jehovah thy God blesseth thee:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and thou shalt rejoice before Jehovah thy God, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy maid-servant, and the Levite that is within thy gates, and the sojourner, and the fatherless, and the widow, that are in the midst of thee, in the place which Jehovah thy God shall choose, to cause his name to dwell there.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And thou shalt remember that thou wast a bondman in Egypt: and thou shalt observe and do these statutes.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou shalt keep the feast of tabernacles seven days, after that thou hast gathered in from thy threshing-floor and from thy winepress:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and thou shalt rejoice in thy feast, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy maid-servant, and the Levite, and the sojourner, and the fatherless, and the widow, that are within thy gates.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Seven days shalt thou keep a feast unto Jehovah thy God in the place which Jehovah shall choose; because Jehovah thy God will bless thee in all thine increase, and in all the work of thy hands, and thou shalt be altogether joyful.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Three times in a year shall all thy males appear before Jehovah thy God in the place which he shall choose: in the feast of unleavened bread, and in the feast of weeks, and in the feast of tabernacles; and they shall not appear before Jehovah empty:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: every man shall give as he is able, according to the blessing of Jehovah thy God which he hath given thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Judges and officers shalt thou make thee in all thy gates, which Jehovah thy God giveth thee, according to thy tribes; and they shall judge the people with righteous judgment.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou shalt not wrest justice: thou shalt not respect persons; neither shalt thou take a bribe; for a bribe doth blind the eyes of the wise, and pervert the words of the righteous.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That which is altogether just shalt thou follow, that thou mayest live, and inherit the land which Jehovah thy God giveth thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou shalt not plant thee an Asherah of any kind of tree beside the altar of Jehovah thy God, which thou shalt make thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Neither shalt thou set thee up a pillar; which Jehovah thy God hateth.
